context.analyste: MedSecure CISO + Healthcare cybersecurity consulting firm
context.reglementation: GDPR, MDR (EU 2017/745), HDS (Health Data Hosting), NIS2 (important entity — healthcare sector)
context.commentaires: Analysis conducted as part of the ISO 27001 initiative and NIS2 compliance effort. Medical devices (sensors, pumps) communicate via Azure IoT Hub to the AWS cloud telemonitoring platform. Health data (HDS) hosted by a certified HDS provider. Critical concern: patient safety.

gravity_scale.label: Critical
gravity_scale.description: Endangerment of patient lives or irreversible loss
gravity_scale.impact_reputation: National/international media coverage, crisis of confidence
gravity_scale.impact_reglementaire: CE/MDR certification withdrawal, major CNIL/ANSM sanction
gravity_scale.impact_operationnel: Medical device unavailability > 4h in critical situation

gravity_scale.label: Severe
gravity_scale.impact_reputation: Loss of trust from several client healthcare facilities
gravity_scale.impact_reglementaire: CNIL formal notice, ANSM medical device vigilance incident

gravity_scale.label: Significant
gravity_scale.description: Service degradation without direct patient impact
gravity_scale.impact_reputation: Client dissatisfaction, healthcare sector media incident

vm.description: Cloud platform for real-time collection and analysis of physiological data from patients equipped with MedSecure devices. Automatic alerts to caregivers. Critical availability: an interruption can delay the detection of a life-threatening emergency.

Physiological data (ECG, SpO2, blood glucose, infusion rate), patient identification data, medical history transmitted by healthcare facilities. Subject to GDPR + HDS. Sensitive data under Article 9 of the GDPR.
Firmware code for physiological sensors and infusion pumps. Classified as medical device software (SaMD/SiMD). Subject to MDR validation. Major intellectual property.
Azure IoT Hub, device authentication certificates, MQTT/AMQP protocols. Management of ~5,000 devices deployed across 50 healthcare facilities.
MDR technical documentation (CE marking file), validation reports, HDS compliance evidence, GDPR processing register. Stored on SharePoint.
CRM, contracts with healthcare facilities, maintenance SLAs, support tickets. Contact data for healthcare professionals.
Patents, physiological anomaly detection algorithms, ML models for critical event prediction. Source code on GitLab. Differentiating competitive advantage.
Accounting (SAP B1), payroll, employee personal data. Billing for client healthcare facilities.

srov.justification: Healthcare sector = priority ransomware target (data value, life-threatening pressure)
Health data = high value on the dark web (10-50x banking data)
Complex IoT supply chain — Chinese components, third-party firmware
Medical devices = media target for researchers (BlackHat, DEF CON)
Potentially sensitive patients (politicians, military) in public hospitals
Firmware developers have access to critical code and signing keys
End users = healthcare staff not trained in cybersecurity
IoT devices potentially exposed on poorly segmented hospital network
Some devices have a patient interface (infusion pump)

ov_list.nom: Theft and resale of health data
Theft of detection algorithms and patents
Firmware compromise via component or update
Espionage of patient data (public figures, military)

sop_summary.ss: SS-07 - IP theft by malicious developer

measures.mesure: MFA Entra ID on all cloud access
measures.details: Deploy multi-factor authentication (MFA) via Microsoft Entra ID across all MedSecure cloud access, including AWS Console, Azure IoT Hub, GitLab, and Microsoft 365. MFA enrollment must be mandatory using phishing-resistant methods (FIDO2, Microsoft Authenticator) in compliance with HDS and ISO 27001 A.8.5 requirements. This measure reduces the risk of account compromise through credential stuffing or phishing by over 99%, thereby protecting access to patient health data.

measures.mesure: Defender for Office 365 (anti-phishing, safe links)
measures.details: Enable Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Plan 2 with advanced anti-phishing capabilities, Safe Links, and Safe Attachments across all MedSecure mailboxes. Configure detection policies specific to the healthcare sector (decoys impersonating ANSM, client healthcare facilities, or MDR notified bodies). This protection significantly reduces the risk of initial compromise through spear-phishing, the primary attack vector against medical device manufacturers.

measures.mesure: SIEM Elastic + outsourced SOC (CloudTrail, Azure logs)
measures.details: Centralize security log collection and analysis in an Elastic SIEM, fed by AWS CloudTrail, Azure Activity Logs, Entra ID journals, and CrowdStrike alerts. The outsourced SOC provides 24/7 monitoring with detection rules tailored to the IoMT context (abnormal access attempts to telemonitoring APIs, unauthorized IoT Hub configuration changes). The target mean time to detect (MTTD) is under 15 minutes for critical alerts impacting patient safety.

measures.mesure: SonarQube SAST in CI/CD (cloud + firmware)
measures.details: Integrate SonarQube into GitLab CI/CD pipelines for static application security testing (SAST) across all cloud application code and embedded medical device firmware. Analysis rules must cover OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, embedded system-specific flaws (buffer overflow, integer overflow), and IEC 62443 requirements. The pipeline automatically blocks any merge request containing critical or high severity vulnerabilities.

measures.mesure: RDS versioning backups + S3 immutability
measures.details: Implement automated PostgreSQL/RDS backups with versioning enabled and 90-day retention in compliance with HDS requirements. Patient data backups are stored in S3 buckets with Object Lock immutability to prevent deletion or encryption by ransomware. Monthly restoration tests are performed with a target RPO of 1 hour and RTO of 4 hours for the telemonitoring platform.

measures.mesure: AES-256 encryption of patient data at rest + TLS 1.3 in transit
measures.details: Systematically encrypt all patient health data at rest with AES-256 (RDS encryption, EBS encryption, S3 SSE-KMS) and in transit with TLS 1.3 for REST APIs and MQTT over TLS for IoT communications. Encryption keys are managed via AWS KMS with automatic annual rotation, in compliance with HDS and GDPR Article 32 requirements. This measure ensures the confidentiality of physiological data (ECG, SpO2, blood glucose) even in the event of unauthorized storage access.

measures.mesure: AWS VPC segmentation + office VLAN
measures.details: Segment the AWS infrastructure into dedicated VPCs (telemonitoring production, development, administration) with restrictive security groups and NACLs. The office network is segmented into VLANs (user workstations, administration, guests). Traffic between segments is filtered and logged, limiting lateral propagation in case of component compromise. This architecture follows the defense-in-depth principle required by ISO 27001 and NIS2.

measures.mesure: Branch protection + mandatory code review GitLab
measures.details: Configure branch protection on GitLab for firmware and cloud platform repositories: mandatory merge requests, minimum two approvers including one security reviewer, and validated CI/CD pipelines before merge. This measure prevents malicious code injection into medical device firmware (SaMD/SiMD) and ensures the change traceability required by the MDR regulation (EU 2017/745) for the technical file.

measures.mesure: Firmware security testing (fuzzing + annual pentests)
measures.details: Conduct thorough security testing on physiological sensor and infusion pump firmware: automated fuzzing of MQTT/BLE interfaces, annual penetration tests by a firm specializing in medical device security, and vulnerability analysis compliant with IEC 62443. Results feed the MDR technical file and the ISO 14971 risk management process. The objective is to identify and remediate vulnerabilities before deployment in hospital environments.

measures.mesure: Centralized SIEM: integrate IoT + Azure + device maintenance logs
measures.details: Extend the Elastic SIEM scope to integrate IoT device logs (MQTT messages, Azure IoT Hub events, provisioning journals), field maintenance traces, and sensor anomalous behavior alerts. Develop specific correlation rules: detection of unsigned firmware, communication to unknown endpoints, unauthorized re-provisioning attempts. This extension covers a critical blind spot for detecting attacks targeting the 5,000 devices deployed across healthcare facilities.
measures.sop: SOP-01 - Ransomware, SOP-02 - Exfiltration, SOP-03 - Supply chain, SOP-06 - PKI

measures.mesure: IoT network segmentation: client deployment guide + monitoring
measures.details: Develop a network deployment guide for the 50 client healthcare facilities, mandating isolation of MedSecure devices in a dedicated VLAN with strict traffic filtering (outbound MQTT/TLS to Azure IoT Hub only). Provide an automated network compliance verification tool for pre-installation checks. Deploy inter-VLAN traffic monitoring to detect any lateral movement attempt from a compromised device to the hospital network.

measures.mesure: Hardened WAF + API rate limiting + automated DAST
measures.details: Harden the AWS WAF protecting the telemonitoring platform APIs with custom rules against specific attacks (SQL injection, XXE, SSRF targeting patient data endpoints). Implement per-facility and per-device API rate limiting to prevent API abuse. Integrate an automated DAST scanner (OWASP ZAP) into the CI/CD pipeline to detect REST API vulnerabilities before each production deployment.

measures.mesure: DLP Microsoft 365 (health data)
measures.details: Deploy Microsoft 365 DLP policies to detect and block unauthorized sharing of health data via email, SharePoint, and Teams. Configure sensitive classifiers for medical data (patient identifiers, ECG/SpO2 results, identification data) and blocking rules for transfers to unauthorized external domains. This measure addresses GDPR sensitive data protection requirements (Article 9) and prevents exfiltration via collaboration tools.

measures.mesure: Hardened PKI key protection: HSM multi-approval + rotation
measures.details: Migrate firmware signing keys to a FIPS 140-2 Level 3 certified HSM (AWS CloudHSM) with a multi-approval mechanism requiring at least two authorized persons (CTO + CISO) for any signing operation. Implement automatic rotation of encryption and signing keys with a maximum period of 12 months. This measure protects medical device firmware integrity against a PKI compromise that could enable injection of malicious updates onto infusion pumps.

measures.mesure: Enhanced healthcare cybersecurity awareness (quarterly + simulation)
measures.details: Establish a quarterly awareness program tailored to the healthcare context, including realistic phishing simulations (impersonation of client facilities, fake ANSM recalls, fake MDR notified body emails). Specifically train biomedical and customer support teams on social engineering risks. Measure click-through and reporting rates with a target click rate below 5% and a reporting rate above 70%.

measures.mesure: Automated health data classification (Azure Information Protection)
measures.details: Deploy Azure Information Protection to automatically classify documents containing health data (patient records, telemonitoring reports, physiological analysis results) stored on SharePoint and exchanged via Microsoft 365. Apply sensitivity labels with automatic encryption and sharing restrictions for documents classified as 'Health Data - Confidential'. This measure complements the DLP framework (M-13) and meets HDS classification requirements.

measures.mesure: Firmware code secrets audit + rotation
measures.details: Conduct a comprehensive audit of sensor and infusion pump firmware code to identify all plaintext secrets (API keys, certificates, Azure IoT Hub connection credentials) using automated detection tools (GitLeaks, TruffleHog) integrated into the CI/CD pipeline. Migrate all identified secrets to HashiCorp Vault with secure injection at build time. Implement automatic rotation of IoT credentials and alerts upon detection of new secrets in the code.

measures.mesure: Unified SSO Entra ID on all services (SAP, Azure IoT admin)
measures.details: Extend Entra ID SSO coverage to all MedSecure applications, including SAP Business One (via SAML/OIDC), the Azure IoT Hub administration interface, and field maintenance tools. Eliminate all non-federated local accounts to ensure centralized identity management, unified access reviews, and immediate revocation upon employee departure. This consolidation strengthens ISO 27001 A.5.15 compliance and simplifies access auditing for HDS.

measures.mesure: Refined Azure IoT Hub RBAC + quarterly review
measures.details: Review and refine Azure IoT Hub RBAC roles by strictly applying the least privilege principle: separate provisioning, monitoring, maintenance, and administration roles. Reduce the number of administrators from 12 to a maximum of 4 and assign granular roles (IoT Hub Data Reader, Device Provisioning Operator). Institute a quarterly access rights review validated by the CISO, in compliance with ISO 27001 A.5.18 and NIS2 requirements.

measures.mesure: Secure firmware SDLC: threat modeling, security review per MDR release
measures.details: Formalize a secure development lifecycle (SDLC) specific to medical device firmware, integrating STRIDE threat modeling at each release, a mandatory security review by the CISO before any MDR notified body submission, and security criteria in validation gates. The process must comply with IEC 62443-4-1 (secure development lifecycle) and directly feed the MDR technical file (Annex II of EU regulation 2017/745).
measures.sop: SOP-03 - Supply chain, SOP-04 - IoT vuln

measures.mesure: IoT traffic monitoring: device communication anomaly detection
measures.details: Deploy an IoT traffic monitoring solution specific to MQTT and AMQP flows between medical devices and Azure IoT Hub, capable of detecting communication anomalies: abnormal data volume, connection attempts from unknown IPs, telemetry pattern changes, and messages not conforming to the expected schema. Integrate these alerts into the SIEM (M-10) with correlation rules adapted to the normal behavior of physiological sensors and infusion pumps.

measures.mesure: Bastion/PAM for cloud and IoT admin access
measures.details: Deploy a bastion/PAM (Privileged Access Management) solution to secure and trace all administrative access to AWS cloud infrastructure, Azure IoT Hub, and medical devices under maintenance. Each administration session must be recorded (video + commands), subject to prior approval for critical actions, and time-limited (4-hour maximum sessions). This measure addresses a critical gap identified in the ANSSI baseline (#29) and meets HDS traceability requirements.

measures.mesure: Secure firmware update process: accelerated critical patch validation
measures.details: Define an accelerated validation and deployment process for critical security patches on medical device firmware, reducing the current 3-6 month delay to under 30 days for CVSS >= 9.0 vulnerabilities. This process includes a streamlined MDR validation procedure for security-only patches (no functional changes), pre-approved by the notified body. Deployment is performed via the OTA server with integrity verification and automatic rollback on failure.
measures.sop: SOP-03 - Supply chain, SOP-04 - IoT vuln
measures.phase: MDR patch cycle too long (3-6 months)

measures.mesure: IoT component obsolescence management plan
measures.details: Establish an obsolescence management plan for electronic components and embedded software in medical devices, with a complete lifecycle inventory (EOL/EOS) of microcontrollers, radio modules, and firmware libraries. Identify components reaching end of support within 24 months and qualify compatible alternatives. This plan feeds the ISO 14971 risk management process and prevents prolonged exposure to unpatched vulnerabilities on deployed devices.

measures.mesure: Supply chain component integrity verification (SBOM + supplier audit)
measures.details: Implement a hardware and software supply chain integrity verification process for medical devices. Generate and maintain a complete SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) for each firmware version in CycloneDX or SPDX format. Audit electronic component suppliers on their security practices (ISO 27001 certification, anti-counterfeiting controls). This measure addresses NIS2 supply chain security requirements and FDA recommendations for connected medical devices.

measures.mesure: Client deployment security guide + pre-installation network audit
measures.details: Develop a comprehensive security guide for healthcare facilities deploying MedSecure devices, covering required network segmentation, firewall rules, Wi-Fi prerequisites, and physical device access policies. Conduct a systematic pre-installation network audit to verify hospital infrastructure compliance with security requirements. Provide architecture recommendations aligned with ANSSI healthcare guidelines and HDS standards for patient data flow hosting.

measures.mesure: Product liability cyber insurance (medical device liability)
measures.details: Subscribe to a cyber insurance policy specifically covering product liability related to connected medical devices, including bodily harm resulting from a cyber-attack on infusion pumps or sensors. Coverage must include GDPR notification costs, MDR batch recall costs, legal defense fees, and business interruption losses. The target coverage amount is a minimum of EUR 10 million, proportionate to the vital patient risk and MDR regulation requirements for Class IIb manufacturers.
measures.effet: Residual risk transfer — medical device manufacturer liability
